actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (bent arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand in the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean forward slightly with your hands gripping the handles.
2.Keep your arms bent, initiating the pull by engaging your core. Swing your arms back past your thighs.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the slider to the highest position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at your hips with a slight bend in your knees, and grip the handles with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Knees bend and extend in a fluid motion, while your hips sit back and down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down to your hips, engage your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.\n2.Hold the ski handles with both hands, keeping them no higher than shoulder level.\n3.Bend your elbows and pull the ropes down to your sides with force.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671815181.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your arms close to your body and maintain good balance throughout.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671818931.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale while pulling, inhale when returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down toward your hips, activate your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.

actionLibraryList.title: Rolling Shrugs
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, arms relaxed.
2.Lead the movement with your shoulder blades, circling up, back, down, and forward.
3.Complete a full circle, then reverse direction.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750400914511.jpg","context":"1.Engage your core.\n2.Lead the movement with your shoulder blades."}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the torso stable, avoiding swinging or using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Shoulder Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Maintain a neutral spine; avoid leaning forward or backward.
2.Keep shoulders relaxed and down; avoid shrugging.
3.Perform the stretch slowly and controlled; do not use momentum.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Bring your arm across your body and hold it straight.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Use your other arm to pull your elbow toward your chest.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: You should feel a strong and intense stretch in your posterior deltoid.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Bring one arm across your body and hold it straight.\n2.Pull your elbow toward your chest with the other arm to deepen the stretch.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748513898963.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel your posterior deltoid getting a strong and intense stretch.

actionLibraryList.title: Pulse Squat to Alternating Hip Abduction
actionLibraryList.context:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core. Lower into a half squat, keeping your thighs at about a 45-degree angle to the floor. Keep your knees stable, hips back, and chest lifted—don’t round your back.
While holding the half squat, bend one knee and lift the leg out to the side into a hip abduction. Control the motion and avoid leaning or rotating your pelvis. Return slowly, then repeat on the other side. Keep your core tight and movements smooth throughout.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751443530145.jpg","context":"1.The leg is above horizontal"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751443534988.jpg","context":"1.Keep the supporting knee in line with the toes"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert, inhale on the return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: The glutes and legs should be actively engaged.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Maintain core stability and smooth movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Front Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Rest the barbell on your front delts and cross your arms to hold it securely.
2.Lift your elbows so your upper arms are slightly above parallel to the ground.
3.Keep your head up, chest out, back straight, and core tight for stability.
4.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes pointing forward or slightly outward.
5.Push your hips back to lower into the squat, keeping your back straight.
6.Maintain a steady center of balance as you rise back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Find a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Do not round your back, and make sure your knees are in line with your toes when squatting.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Maintain smooth breathing, inhale as you squat, exhale as you press upward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your quadriceps, press back into the starting stance.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider.
2.Cross your arms to hold the barbell securely with your elbows lifted.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230472738.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor, keeping your back straight.
2.Keep your knees aligned with your toes and avoid letting them cave inward.
3.Turn your feet slightly outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230476871.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your quadriceps to return your body to a neutral standing position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your back straight and align your knees with your toes as you squat down.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than hip-width.
2.Keep your shoulders back, core engaged, and knees slightly bent.
3.Begin driving your hips backward.
4.Avoid excessive knee bending or losing proper posture in your upper body.
5.Continue bending, feeling the stretch in your hamstrings.
6.Pause briefly at the bottom position, then drive your hips forward to return to standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the floor with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start by establishing a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Hinge backward, keeping your back straight, and feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Maintain smooth breathing throughout. Inhale as you lower yourself, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Hinge at your hips to fully stretch your hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Relax your shoulders.
2.Keep your back straight and glutes engaged.
3.Slightly bend your knees.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749439326943.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.
2.Slightly bend your knees, keeping the barbell close to your body.
3.Feet hip-width apart.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749439330202.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a full stretch in your hamstrings as you hinge forward.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees only slightly bent.

actionLibraryList.title: Forearm Plank Cable Pull
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the machine mount to knee height and attach the handle to the mount.
2.Set up in a high plank with your feet away from the machine and your hands near the edge of the base.
3.Grab the handle with one hand, keeping your body tight and your eyes looking down.
4.Pull the handle towards you, bringing your elbow towards your hip.
5.The goal is to keep your body stabilized through your core and perform controlled movement.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the handle to a mid-low position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Hold a high plank position and grasp the handle in your hand.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Pull the handle backward while keeping your body stable.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Make sure you tuck your elbow tight to your side.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you pull the handle backward and inhale as you release.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Brace your core throughout the movement and squeeze your lats as you pull the handle backward.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Grab the handle with one hand, keeping your body tight and eyes down.\n2.Keep your body stabilized through your core.\n3.The goal is to control the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750930051905.jpg"},{"context":"Pull the handle backward until your elbow is close to your torso.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750930055901.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you pull the handle backward and inhale as you release.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Brace your core throughout the movement and squeeze your lats as you pull the handle backward.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Make sure you tuck your elbow tight to your side.

actionLibraryList.title: Burpees
actionLibraryList.context: Quickly switch between each phase while maintaining body stability, and keep your core engaged throughout to protect your lower back.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Perform a push-up, then explosively jump up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Drop your legs into a push-up position. Perform a push-up, then return your legs and step up, extending your arms overhead as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Initiate the jump-into-the-air movement by engaging your abdominal muscles.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Brace your core and engage your abdominal muscles.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Lower your chest until it nearly touches the ground.
2.Keep your elbows flared out to the sides.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750041773473.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750041776935.jpg","context":"1.Slightly bend your knees to absorb the impact upon landing.
2.Keep your core tight throughout the jump.
"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Brace your core and engage your abdominal muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Initiate the jump-into-the-air movement by engaging your abdominal muscles.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Thruster
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the machine handles on the floor mounts.
2.Grip the handles with a parallel grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
3.Stand with your feet slightly wider than hip-width apart, with toes pointing slightly outward.
4.Keeping your chest up, push your knees forward and your hips slightly back.
5.Lower your body with the goal of sitting between your feet.
6.Lower until your quads are just below horizontal.
7.Drive through your heels to stand up while simultaneously initiating a shoulder press.
8.Start with the handles at shoulder level, elbows pointed downward.
9.Press the handles upward to fully extend your arms overhead.
10.Try to avoid shrugging your shoulders at the top position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Secure the pulley block to the footboard end with the two handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and your toes slightly pointing outward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Grab a pair of handles and press them overhead.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Try to extend your arm fully, but avoid locking your elbows.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Inhale as you lower your body into a squat, and exhale as you rise to a standing position and press the weights overhead.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your core and keep it tight throughout to maintain stability.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Grip the handles with a parallel grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2.Keep the chest up, push the knees forward, and shift the hips slightly back.
3.Lower yourself until the thighs are just below parallel to the ground.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750239293831.jpg"},{"context":"1.Push the handles upward to fully extend the arms overhead.
2.Stand with your feet slightly wider than hip-width apart, with toes pointing slightly outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750239297328.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower your body into a squat, and exhale as you rise to a standing position and press the weights overhead.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your core and keep it tight throughout to maintain stability.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Try to extend your arm fully, but avoid locking your elbows.

actionLibraryList.title: Mountain Climbers
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your body in a straight line.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Don't hold your breath, keep your breathing steady.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Bring your knees forward by engaging your abdomen.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Support your body with both hands.\n2.Lift your knees and alternately touch your elbows.\n3.Engage your core and avoid letting your hips sag.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750043526151.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Bring your knees forward by engaging your abdomen.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your body in a straight line.
